title: Module 6.6: NOTAM Procedures & Emergency Protocols - Assessment
description: Assessment of knowledge for NOTAM procedures and emergency response

questions.text: What is the exposure limit for visible laser light in the Laser-Free Zone (LFZ)?
questions.explanation: The Laser-Free Zone limit is 50 nW/cm² (nanowatts per square centimeter), the most restrictive of the flight zones.

questions.text: How quickly must the emergency stop be activated after an observer reports an aircraft?
questions.explanation: The emergency stop must be activated IMMEDIATELY - within 2 seconds is considered the maximum acceptable response time.

questions.text: What does 'LAS' mean in a NOTAM?
questions.explanation: 'LAS' in a NOTAM indicates laser activity. It is the keyword that alerts pilots to laser operations in the area.

questions.text: On FAA Form 7140-1, what value should be entered in Section 12b (MPE) for a continuous wave visible laser?
questions.explanation: For continuous wave visible lasers (400-700nm), the MPE value is 2.6 mW/cm² for 0.25 second exposure.

questions.text: Who should be contacted FIRST if a pilot reports being illuminated by a laser during your show?
questions.explanation: The FAA and ATC should be contacted first for any laser illumination incident. They will coordinate with law enforcement if needed.

questions.text: During an emergency shutdown, in what order should actions be taken?

options.text: Contact ATC, verify shutdown, document, assess restart
Activate emergency stop, verify all beams off, contact ATC, await all-clear
Document incident, shutdown equipment, notify promoter, contact FAA
Assess situation, contact supervisor, shutdown if approved, file report

questions.explanation: The correct sequence is: immediate emergency stop, verify shutdown, contact ATC, await all-clear before any restart consideration.

questions.text: What is the formula for calculating CZED (Critical Zone Exposure Distance) from SZED?
questions.explanation: CZED = SZED × 4.47. The LFED is SZED × 44.7. These relationships are constant multipliers.

questions.text: A police helicopter is investigating a report and flies through your protected airspace. You did NOT illuminate them. What should you do?

options.text: Continue the show - you're not involved
Immediately shutdown, verify beams are off, document the incursion
Contact police and explain you're authorized
Redirect beams away from the helicopter

questions.explanation: Regardless of whether you caused the report, any aircraft incursion requires immediate shutdown to ensure safety and demonstrate responsibility.

questions.text: How long should a NOTAM remain active after the laser operation concludes?

options.text: Until the end of the day

questions.explanation: NOTAMs are issued for specific date/time ranges and automatically expire. They should only be active during actual operation times.

questions.text: If the NOHD calculation is LESS than the SZED for a visible laser, which distance determines the safety perimeter?

options.text: NOHD - eye injury is the primary concern
The average of NOHD and SZED
The longer of the two distances

questions.explanation: When NOHD < SZED, enter 'Less than NOHD' in SZED cells. The NOHD is always the primary safety distance for eye protection.

questions.text: Which of the following are triggers for immediate emergency shutdown? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: All of these except promoter request are valid shutdown triggers. Safety always takes priority over show continuation.

questions.text: A NOTAM can be filed on the same day as the laser operation.
questions.explanation: NOTAMs should be filed at least 3 days in advance. Same-day filing is not acceptable for planned operations.

questions.text: The conversion factor used to calculate NOHD slant range in feet is ______.
questions.explanation: The constant 32.8 converts centimeters to feet and radians to milliradians in the NOHD formula.

questions.text: Following an emergency shutdown, when can you resume operations?

options.text: After 5 minutes regardless of aircraft status
Only after all observers confirm airspace clear

questions.explanation: All observers must confirm clear airspace before resuming. ATC notification is good practice but observer confirmation is mandatory.

questions.text: Match the protection distance with its description:
questions.explanation: Understanding the four protection distances is fundamental to completing FAA Form 7140-1 correctly.
